etymology.description: semantic 亻(人) (rén, "person") + phonetic 壬(壬) (rén)
etymology.interpretation: The character 任(rèn, "duty; appoint") combines the radical 亻(亻) (rén, "person") to convey the notion of an individual bearing responsibility, with the phonetic component 壬(壬) (rén), which originally depicted a shoulder pole and provided the sound. This association with a shoulder pole suggests the act of bearing a burden, linking conceptually to taking on a duty or role. This further extends to the sense of 'appoint', as appointing someone involves designating them to bear certain responsibilities.

examples.chinese: 他被任命为部门经理(他被任命為部門經理)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bèi rènmìng wèi bùmén jīnglǐ.
examples.english: He was appointed as the department manager.

examples.chinese: 你有什么职责(你有甚麼職責)？
她在工作中尽职尽责(她在工作中盡職盡責)，被大家认可(被大家認可)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài gōngzuò zhōng jìnzhí jìnzé, bèi dàjiā rènkě.
examples.english: She is conscientious in her work and is acknowledged by everyone.
